heroHeadline: Kenya MSS Mission Formally Ends Apr 28; Haiti Security Gap Widens as GSF Builds From ~545 Personnel to Full 5,500 Strength
heroSubtitle: Kenya's final 150 Multinational Security Support Mission officers departed Haiti on April 28, 2026, formally ending the 22-month Kenya-led mission established under UN Security Council Resolution 2699. The withdrawal completes a phased drawdown begun in March 2026, leaving Haiti's security during the transition in the hands of the Gang Suppression Force advance contingent of approximately 545 personnel — 400 Chadian troops, 75 Guatemalan police, and 70 Salvadoran police. Despite the MSS securing Toussaint-Louverture Airport and facilitating the TPC installation in April 2024, gang territory in Port-au-Prince expanded from approximately 80% to approximately 90% of the capital over the mission's 22-month lifespan. Security analysts and Haiti's National Police Director General warn that the transition period represents the highest vulnerability window since the MSS deployed — particularly in Croix-des-Bouquets, Delmas, and downtown Port-au-Prince — until the full 5,500-person GSF force reaches operational capacity projected for fall–end 2026. Separately, the US Supreme Court heard oral arguments on April 27 in the consolidated TPS cases Noem v. Doe / Trump v. Miot, with a ruling expected by late June or early July 2026 that will determine the fate of approximately 350,000 Haitian TPS holders in the United States; the House-passed H.R. 1689 three-year TPS extension faces Senate Republican opposition and a threatened White House veto. The 2026 Humanitarian Response Plan remains only approximately 20% funded ($172M of $880M needed), with 5.8 million Haitians (52% of the population) facing IPC Phase 3+ food insecurity through June 2026.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, Jamaica Gleaner, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre, ICI Haiti, JURIST, International Rescue Committee, NPR, NBC News, Capital FM Kenya, Dominican Today, Security Council Report, UN News, Xinhua, Haiti Sun, SCOTUSblog, Senate Democratic Leadership, WLRN / NPR South Florida, FANM, Haitian Bridge Alliance, ACLU, The Star Kenya, Local10 / WPLG Miami, Washington Post, Lehigh Valley Immigration Lawyers, CGTN Africa, Kahawa Tungu, Mwakilishi
